rulings.Case Title: LEVY HUGHES JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Quashing Defendant Channel Lumber Company' Request For Admissions, Set Two And Special Interrogatories, Set Two And Request For Production, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 11, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion for protective order quashing defendant Channel Lumber Co.¿s request for admissions, set two and special interrogatories, set two and request for production, set two. Hearing required. At the hearing each side should be prepared to state each issue that needs to be resolved and why the issue should be resolved in that side¿s favor. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN SOTELO VS. AMCORD, INC.(INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Union Carbide Corporations Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 11, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of Union Carbide Corp.¿s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records is denied. Plaintiffs have not shown that there are any questions they wish to ask Mr. Myers that have not been adequately covered in or more of the depositions of Mr. Myers included in the papers filed on this motion. The revised document requests are over broad and not likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN SOTELO VS. AMCORD, INC.(INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For A Protective Order Prohibiting (1) The Taking Of The Depositions Of Person Most Knowledgeable/Custodian Of Record; And (2) The Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, May 11, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Union Carbide Corp.¿s motion for a protective order prohibiting (1) the taking of the depositions of person most knowledgeable/custodian of records, and (2) the production of documents is granted. Plaintiffs have not shown that there are any questions they wish to ask Mr. Myers that have not been adequately covered in or more of the depositions of Mr. Myers included in the papers filed on this motion. The revised document requests are over broad and not likely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. = (220/HEK)
